title: Backtested my NQ futures bot on 3 years instead of 7 months — discovered the 74% WR / PF 2.38 headline was a recovery-window cherry-pick. Built a replacement that holds up out-of-sample.
author: ToughAssignment3869
selftext:
I had an NQ futures bot running live on Tradovate with what looked like a strong backtest:



\- 7-month window: \*\***74.6% WR, PF 2.38, +$10,499 net**\*\*



Then I ran the same strategy on 3 years of Databento NQ continuous data (Jan 2023 → May 2026, 235k 5-min bars):



\`\`\`

ATB D3 — full 3-year backtest:

  Trades:        332

  Win Rate:      61.1%

  Profit Factor: 1.20      ← marginal, not 2.38

  Net P&L:       +$8,305   ← \~$2,500/year average

  Max DD:        -$6,010   ← 72% of total profit

  Underwater:    Jan 2023 → Nov 2025 (35 months)

\`\`\`



The 7-month "success window" was literally the recovery period after 2.5 years of bleeding. Cherry-pick.



\*\***Stopped the bot the same day**\*\*, then went searching for a strategy with real out-of-sample edge. Found one — \`Strategy B\` from ICT multi-timeframe FVG research on the same dataset:



\`\`\`

Strategy B — 5-min Inverse FVG inside 15-min FVG (long-only):

  Trades:        432 over 3 years (\~140/year, \~2-3/wk)

  Win Rate:      53.5%

  Profit Factor: \~2.3

  Net P&L:       +$17,187 on 1 MNQ

  Max DD:        only $786 over the full period

  Return / DD:   \~22x

  Profitable years:    4/4

  Profitable quarters: 13/14 (93% hit rate)

\`\`\`



Now running on Tradovate demo for 2-week validation before any live capital.



\*\***Both repos public (equity curves embedded in each README):**\*\*



\- \[nq-atb-bot-archived\](https://github.com/prashanthaitha24/nq-atb-bot-archived) — the abandoned ATB with the honest backtest, published as a "don't do this" reference

\- \[nq-strategy-b-bot\](https://github.com/prashanthaitha24/nq-strategy-b-bot) — the replacement with the multi-timeframe FVG logic



The key insight: ATB is a breakout that needs trending markets. Strategy B requires \*\***price to dip into a 5-min Bull FVG that's INSIDE an active 15-min Bull FVG, then close back above**\*\* — a structural confluence that genuinely selects for follow-through. That's a real edge; ATB was just a breakout that worked in 2025.



\*\***Lessons learned (might save someone else):**\*\*



1. \*\***Always backtest 2-3+ years**\*\* on futures. Anything shorter is regime-specific.

2. \*\***Compare strategies on identical data**\*\*. The "Strategy B beats ATB" comparison is meaningful because it's the same bars, same window, same fees.

3. \*\***Save the loser's code.**\*\* Archived ATB to GitHub publicly with the honest numbers — useful as a "don't do this" reference for myself + others.

4. \*\***Walk-forward / OOS is the only honest test.**\*\* I also tested 16 different filter combos on ATB to "fix" it. NONE survived out-of-sample. The losing strategy was just a losing strategy; no filter rescues it.



Happy to discuss the methodology or the implementation. Both repos include the data + backtest scripts so you can reproduce both results.

comments.author: AffectionateDrop2155
comments.body: nq slippage and movement is monsterous. would hope for a profit far above $2500/year for those kinds of risks and commissions

replies.author: ToughAssignment3869
replies.body:
100%, that's exactly why I stopped running it. $2,500/year on $6K max DD with NQ's slippage profile is not a tradable risk-adjusted return; that's basically renting capital to the broker.

  The replacement (Strategy B in the post) does \~$5K/year per MNQ with $786 max DD over the full 3 years on the same dataset — different math entirely (Return/DD \~22× vs ATB's 1.4×). And those numbers are 1 MNQ, so scale linearly with contracts once it's proven in live.

 Real-world slippage on MNQ at 5-min bar opens has been running about 0.25-0.5 ticks per side in my live demo so far, which the backtest already bakes in at 0.5 tick + $1.18 RT commission. NQ proper will be worse — wouldn't scale up until I have 6+ months of live data showing the slippage assumption holds.
